business_rationale: Under tight customer delivery deadlines, splitting the total purchase amount into smaller chunks can speed up the approval process by requiring only manager-level approvals, which have shorter lead times than department_head approvals.
control_concern: Splitting purchase requests to bypass amount-based approval thresholds weakens the Amount-based approval control (P2P-C-001), potentially leading to insufficient review and increased risk of inappropriate spending.
expected_effect: The total purchase is approved faster but circumventing the intended control that higher-value purchases require higher-level approvals; this could lead to inadequate oversight of large expenditures.
proposed_behavior: Requester creates multiple smaller purchase requests just below the department_head approval threshold (e.g., multiple requests each slightly under 1,000,000) to expedite approvals and reduce lead time under delivery deadline pressure.
review_evidence: Audit trail of multiple purchase requests from the same requester, vendor, and project with amounts just below 1,000,000 within a short time frame.
Approval logs showing manager-level approvals for these multiple smaller requests instead of department_head approvals.
Comparison of timestamps indicating expedited approvals compared to historical full-amount requests.
